title: neotinea lactea subsp. corsica
intro: Neotinea lactea subsp. corsica, also known as Corsican summer orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Neotinea lactea orchid, native to the island of Corsica. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Neotinea lactea subsp. corsica include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability and use are strictly regulated due to its endangered status.
